Ad Text:
Our Story 🚀


At Safewill, we’re transforming estate planning and end-of-life services through beautifully simple, accessible technology. Backed by investors like King River Capital, Westpac’s Reinventure Fund, Carthona Capital and Flying Fox Ventures, we’re building a digital ecosystem that makes estate planning more affordable, more personalised, and more relevant for modern Australians.

Now we’re looking for a Principal UX Researcher to help shape what comes next.

This is a high-impact role at the intersection of product, research and strategy - where you’ll drive discovery, shape direction, and influence what we build.

Your Story with Us 🤩


This is not a support function.

You’ll be embedded in product squads, helping define the problems we solve - not just validating solutions.

You’ll operate with a high level of ownership, partnering closely with Product, Design, Engineering, CX and Leadership to ensure decisions are grounded in strong insight.

What You’ll Work On

Drive discovery and turn insights into clear problems, opportunities and product direction

Own the research strategy across key domains, ensuring we focus on the highest-impact questions

Design and run end-to-end research, from early exploration through to evaluative work

Combine qualitative and quantitative methods to build a deep understanding of customer behaviour

Shape product strategy and roadmap through evidence, linking insight to business outcomes

Define success measures and ensure we’re learning from what we ship

Synthesize research, data and market signals into clear narratives that influence decisions

Facilitate workshops and align teams around insight-led direction

Improve how we do research and embed strong discovery practices across teams

What You'll Bring Across

You’re an insight-driven product thinker.

You go beyond research execution - you shape decisions, influence direction, and bring clarity to complex problems.

You’re comfortable with ambiguity and know how to turn messy inputs into clear, actionable insight.

We’re likely to be a fit if you:

Have 5+ years in UX research, product research, or a similar role

Have worked in startup, scaleup, or B2B2C environments

Are strong across both generative and evaluative research

Know when to go deep and when to move fast

Influence cross-functional teams without relying on authority

Communicate clearly and bring structured thinking to complex problems

Use data, experimentation and research together to guide decisions

Why Safewill? 🤝


Our Product Culture

Product, design and engineering work together from the start - shaping problems, not just delivering solutions. You’ll understand the “why”, help define the “what”, and drive the “how” through to shipped outcomes.

Work stays with the people who shape it. You’ll own discovery end-to-end, with fast feedback loops and real customer insight.

We create space to improve how we build. Dedicated time is set aside to step back, refine our ways of working, and fix the things that slow us down, whether that’s product quality, tooling, or process.

We regularly step outside the roadmap to explore new ideas. Through hackathons and focused sessions, teams test concepts, experiment quickly, and bring the best ideas back into the product.

We share what we learn. From demos to deep-dives, there are regular opportunities to showcase work, explore new approaches, and lift the bar across product, design and engineering.

We live by our SAFE values:

Speed: We move quickly with purpose

All-in: We win as a team

Find the Impact: We focus on what matters

Elevate: We raise the bar, every day

TL;DR We’re rewriting how Australians deal with death by making estate planning simple, accessible and modern through great technology. At Safewill, you’ll grow faster because you’re closer to the action. You’ll see how strategy becomes execution, how data shapes decisions, and how a small team builds something that genuinely changes lives.

I'm partnering with a well-established organisation delivering customer-facing digital solutions within a fast-moving, product-led environment. 

You'll sit within a Continuous Delivery team aligned to the Customer New business stream, working directly on technology that supports sales pipelines and real customer interactions. 

It's hands-on, collaborative, and very much embedded in delivery 
 
Key Responsibilities 

Gather and refine business requirements, including scope and benefits for new initiatives 
Produce high-quality artefacts (activity diagrams, use cases, user stories, acceptance criteria) 
Work closely with engineers on solution design, including reviewing conceptual and sequence diagrams 
Collaborate with QA teams on test plans, test cases, and defect triage 
Support offshore delivery teams and ensure smooth coordination across squads 
 
Required Skills & Experience 

Proven experience as a Business Analyst within digital or technology delivery environments 
Strong capability in writing user stories, epics, and acceptance criteria (Jira) 
Experience producing BA artefacts such as activity diagrams and use cases 
Comfortable working in Agile, cross-functional teams (product, engineering, QA) 
Strong stakeholder engagement skills across both business and technical teams 
 
The Environment 

Product-led, customer-centric delivery model 
Cross-functional squads (Product, Delivery, Engineering, QA) 
Fast-paced, continuous delivery setup with real ownership

Principal Business Analyst for large-scale Government portfolio with multiple long-term projects
Sydney based 12 month contract (potential extension) with WFH hybrid, paying up to $1046 plus Super per day
15+ years of end-to-end enterprise wide project experience required

A Government organisation is seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to join an Enterprise Renewal and Transformation Program. This role is suited to a high-calibre BA who operates beyond traditional requirements gathering-someone who can lead problem definition, shape solutions, and influence delivery outcomes across complex business and technology initiatives.

You will work across a portfolio of strategic projects spanning enterprise system upgrades, integrations, and new capability delivery, partnering closely with Project Managers, technical teams, and business stakeholders. You will be expected to operate with a high degree of autonomy, proactively identifying opportunities for improvement and driving outcomes in a dynamic and evolving environment.

Operating within a structured (waterfall-led) delivery environment, you will play a critical role in ensuring clarity of scope, integrity of requirements, and alignment between business intent and technical execution. You will also act as a key bridge between business and technology, translating complex concepts into clear, actionable insights that support decision-making at all levels.

Key Responsibilities

1. Problem Definition & Scoping

Lead the identification and framing of complex business problems-ensuring clarity of scope, objectives, and success criteria-while challenging assumptions and identifying risks, gaps, and dependencies early in the lifecycle
Facilitate workshops and stakeholder discussions to drive alignment on problem statements and priorities
Apply structured, evidence-based analysis to assess options and recommend practical, value-driven solutions
Contribute to business case development, including cost/benefit analysis and impact assessments where required

2. Requirements & Solution Alignment

Elicit, analyse, and document high-quality business and technical requirements (functional & non-functional), ensuring they are clear, testable, and aligned to delivery outcomes
Maintain traceability across requirements, design, and delivery artefacts to ensure alignment and control
Work closely with architects and technical teams on integration design, data flows, and system interactions
Support prioritisation and scope management, ensuring alignment with program objectives and constraints

3. Process & Operating Model Design

Model current and future-state processes, identifying opportunities for optimisation, simplification, and improved customer outcomes
Ensure process design aligns with system capabilities, governance frameworks, and operational realities
Support definition of business rules, controls, and service delivery models
Contribute to organisational readiness by considering impacts to people, process, and technology

4. Delivery Support & Quality Assurance

Support end-to-end delivery across design, build, testing, and implementation phases, ensuring full traceability from requirements through to solution delivery and testing outcomes
Partner with testing teams to define acceptance criteria, support UAT, and validate that solutions meet business needs
Proactively identify and manage risks, issues, and dependencies, escalating where appropriate
Contribute to governance forums, providing clear and concise reporting on requirements status, risks, and decisions

5. Stakeholder Engagement & Communication

Build and maintain strong relationships with business and technical stakeholders at all levels
Translate complex technical concepts into business-friendly language, and vice versa
Influence decision-making through clear communication, structured analysis, and well-articulated recommendations
Manage competing stakeholder priorities and navigate ambiguity to drive alignment and outcomes
Key Skills & Experience:
Extensive Business Analysis experience (Senior level) within complex enterprise environments, ideally across large-scale transformation or system implementation programs
Proven ability to lead problem definition and shape solutions, not just document requirements-demonstrated experience influencing outcomes at a program or portfolio level
Strong experience working in structured delivery environments (waterfall or hybrid) with a clear understanding of governance, stage gates, and documentation standards
Advanced skills in requirements elicitation, analysis, and documentation, including functional and non-functional requirements, user stories (where applicable), and acceptance criteria
Demonstrated capability in process modelling and service design, including current/future state mapping and identifying optimisation opportunities
Experience working on enterprise systems (e.g. ERP, CRM, asset management, finance, or integration-heavy environments)
Solid understanding of system integrations, data flows, and dependencies, with the ability to engage effectively with architects and technical teams
Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills, with experience working across business and technical audiences, including senior leadership
Proven ability to manage ambiguity, competing priorities, and complex stakeholder environments
Experience supporting testing phases, including defining acceptance criteria and supporting UAT
Highly developed analytical thinking and problem-solving skills, with a pragmatic and outcome-focused approach
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to produce clear, concise documentation and present insights effectively
Experience within government or regulated environments is desirable but not essential

Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples are encouraged to apply.

To apply please click apply or call Lachlan Mitrovich on 02 8289 3173 for a confidential discussion.
